verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a single-pass type I transmembrane protein of the immunoglobulin superfamily expressed specifically in sperm. In the testis/sperm lineage it is present in the acrosomal membrane; upon the acrosome reaction (triggered at fertilization), IZUMO1 relocates to the sperm plasma membrane outer leaflet where it binds its egg-side partner JUNO to mediate gamete fusion. This is the canonical biology: the protein has its own TM anchor and reaches the outer leaflet as part of a narrowly tissue/cell-type-restricted program (sperm, acrosome-reacted state). This maps to contextual/tissue_restricted_surface — the surface display is real and via the protein's own TM domain, but it is restricted to a single germline/reproductive cell type and further requires the acrosome-reaction activation state to fully relocate to the outer PM leaflet.
